title: manihot gracilis subsp. gracilis
intro: Manihot gracilis subsp. gracilis, also known as the bitter cassava or manioc, is a subspecies of the manihot gracilis plant that is native to Central and South America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Manihot gracilis subsp. gracilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. Manihot gracilis subsp. gracilis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in traditional medicine in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
